text: Let $f$ be a continuous function for all real numbers. Let $g$ be the function defined by $g(x)=\displaystyle\int_1^x f(t)\,dt$. If the average rate of change of $g$ on the interval $2\le x\le5$ is $6$, which of the following statements must be true?

options.A: The average value of $f$ on the interval $2\le x\le5$ is $6$.

text:
For any function $f$, which of the following statements must be true?

I. If $f$ is defined at $x=a$, then $\displaystyle\lim_{x\to a}f(x)=f(a)$.

II. If $f$ is continuous at $x=a$, then $\displaystyle\lim_{x\to a}f(x)=f(a)$.

III. If $f$ is differentiable at $x=a$, then $\displaystyle\lim_{x\to a}f(x)=f(a)$.
